source: Ralph Waldo Emerson, Essays: First Series, Self-Reliance (Project Gutenberg #2944)
title: Trust Thyself
why_selected: A compact American statement of inner authority; good for appositions, imperatives, and abstract nouns.
text_en: Trust thyself: every heart vibrates to that iron string. Accept the place the divine providence has found for you, the society of your contemporaries, the connection of events. Great men have always done so, and confided themselves childlike to the genius of their age, betraying their perception that the absolutely trustworthy was seated at their heart, working through their hands, predominating in all their being.

glosses.hint: people living at the same time

scaffold: 信任[祈使] 你自己：每一颗心 震动[一般现在] 到 那根 铁弦。接受[祈使] 那个位置，神意 已经 找到[现在完成 has+pp] 给你，接受[省略祈使] 你的同时代人的社会，接受[省略祈使] 事件的连接。伟大的人 总是 已经 这样 做过[现在完成 have+pp]，并且 把他们自己 像孩子一样 托付[一般过去] 给 他们时代的天才，显露[现在分词 -ing] 他们的感知：那个绝对可信的东西 坐落[一般过去 被动感] 在 他们的心里，工作[现在分词 -ing] 通过 他们的手，在 他们全部存在中 占主导[现在分词 -ing]。

palette.usage: present perfect inside relative-like object phrase
object in a list after Accept
participial phrase explaining the prior clause

grammar: Two imperatives set the passage's command structure: Trust, Accept.
The long third sentence hangs on Great men have done + and confided + betraying.
Working and predominating are participial continuations, not new finite verbs.
note: English lets Emerson pile abstract nouns after imperatives; Chinese needs you to keep the command spine visible.

source: Ralph Waldo Emerson, Essays: First Series, Self-Reliance (Project Gutenberg #2944)
title: A Foolish Consistency
why_selected: A sharp passage about changing one's mind; useful for aphorism, concession, and contrast.
text_en: A foolish consistency is the hobgoblin of little minds, adored by little statesmen and philosophers and divines. With consistency a great soul has simply nothing to do. He may as well concern himself with his shadow on the wall. Speak what you think now in hard words and to-morrow speak what to-morrow thinks in hard words again, though it contradict every thing you said to-day.

glosses.hint: a frightening little spirit; here, a mental bugbear

scaffold: 一种 愚蠢的 一致性 是[一般现在] 小心灵的 妖怪，被 小政治家、小哲学家和神学家 崇拜[过去分词 被动修饰]。对于 一致性，一个 伟大的灵魂 简直 没有什么 要做[一般现在 has nothing to do]。他 也完全可以 让自己 操心[情态 may + 原形] 墙上的影子。说出[祈使] 你 现在 想的东西，用 强硬的话；并且 明天 再说出[祈使] 明天 所想的东西，用 强硬的话，即使 它 矛盾[一般现在] 你 今天 说过[一般过去] 的一切。

palette.usage: has nothing to do with structure

grammar: The first sentence is an aphorism: subject + is + metaphor.
Adored by... is a reduced passive relative clause modifying hobgoblin.
Though introduces permission for contradiction, not an apology for error.
note: The English keeps hitting with short propositions; don't smooth away the shock of hobgoblin and hard words.

title: Live Deliberately
why_selected: A famous passage on reducing life to essentials; excellent for infinitive chains and purpose clauses.
text_en: I went to the woods because I wished to live deliberately, to front only the essential facts of life, and see if I could not learn what it had to teach, and not, when I came to die, discover that I had not lived. I did not wish to live what was not life, living is so dear; nor did I wish to practise resignation, unless it was quite necessary.

glosses.hint: acceptance of something bad as unavoidable

scaffold: 我 去了[一般过去] 到 树林，因为 我 希望[一般过去] 去 有意识地 活，去 只 直面 生活的 本质事实，并且 看看[原形 see] 是否 我 不能 学到[情态 could + 原形] 它 必须 教给[一般过去 had to teach] 我的东西，并且 不要 在 我 来到[一般过去] 死的时候，发现[原形 discover] 我 没有 活过[过去完成 had+pp]。我 不 希望[一般过去] 去 活 那个 不是[一般过去] 生活的东西，活着 是[一般现在] 如此 珍贵；我 也不 希望[一般过去] 去 实践 认命，除非 它 是[一般过去] 相当 必要的。

palette.usage: past perfect; life not truly lived before death

grammar: The first sentence is one long purpose chain after wished: to live, to front, and see, and not discover.
Had not lived is past perfect because the failure would be discovered at death.
Nor did I wish uses auxiliary inversion after a negative connector.
note: Chinese wants to break this into smaller clauses; English lets the purpose chain stay suspended after wished.

title: Quiet Desperation
why_selected: A short diagnostic paragraph about hidden unhappiness; good for passive, contrast, and abstract social language.
text_en: The mass of men lead lives of quiet desperation. What is called resignation is confirmed desperation. From the desperate city you go into the desperate country, and have to console yourself with the bravery of minks and muskrats. A stereotyped but unconscious despair is concealed even under what are called the games and amusements of mankind.
scaffold: 大多数 人 过着[一般现在] 安静绝望的 生活。被 称作[一般现在 被动] 认命的东西 是[一般现在] 被确认的 绝望。从 绝望的 城市 你 走进[一般现在] 绝望的 乡村，并且 不得不 安慰[have to + 原形] 你自己，用 水貂和麝鼠的 勇敢。一个 刻板的 但 无意识的 绝望 被隐藏[一般现在 被动] 甚至 在 那些 被称作[一般现在 被动] 人类的 游戏和娱乐 的东西下面。
grammar: What is called resignation is a subject clause, not a question.
Is concealed is passive; despair is hidden under games and amusements.
From the city into the country is a movement pattern carrying the same adjective desperate.
note: The sentence keeps repeating desperation in different containers; keep that repetition rather than replacing it with synonyms.

source: Frederick Douglass, Narrative of the Life of Frederick Douglass (Project Gutenberg #23)
title: The Silver Trump of Freedom
why_selected: A passage where an abstract idea becomes an all-perceiving presence; useful for metaphor and repeated parallel structure.
text_en: The silver trump of freedom had roused my soul to eternal wakefulness. Freedom now appeared, to disappear no more forever. It was heard in every sound, and seen in every thing. It was ever present to torment me with a sense of my wretched condition. I saw nothing without seeing it, I heard nothing without hearing it, and felt nothing without feeling it.
scaffold: 自由的 银色号角 已经 唤醒[过去完成 had+pp] 我的灵魂 到 永恒的 清醒。自由 现在 显现[一般过去]，为了 不再 消失[不定式 to + 原形] 永远。它 被听见[一般过去 被动] 在 每一个声音里，并且 被看见[一般过去 被动] 在 每一个东西里。它 总是 在场[一般过去] 来 折磨[不定式 to + 原形] 我，用 对 我悲惨处境的 感觉。我 什么也 没有看见[一般过去] 而不 看见[without + -ing] 它，我 什么也 没有听见[一般过去] 而不 听见[without + -ing] 它，并且 什么也 没有感觉[一般过去] 而不 感觉[without + -ing] 它。

palette.usage: past perfect; awakening before the later perception

grammar: Had roused is past perfect; the awakening is already complete before the described state.
Was heard and seen are passives: freedom is perceived everywhere.
The final sentence repeats nothing without + -ing three times; the repetition is the force.
note: The English turns freedom into a sensory object; keep the repeated it instead of explaining it away.

source: Mary Shelley, Frankenstein; or, the Modern Prometheus (Project Gutenberg #84)
title: A Torrent of Light
why_selected: A passage about knowledge, creation, and dangerous ambition; good for modals, relative clauses, and abstract ethical vocabulary.
text_en: No one can conceive the variety of feelings which bore me onwards, like a hurricane, in the first enthusiasm of success. Life and death appeared to me ideal bounds, which I should first break through, and pour a torrent of light into our dark world. A new species would bless me as its creator and source; many happy and excellent natures would owe their being to me.
scaffold: 没有人 能 想象[情态 can + 原形] 那种 感情的 多样性，那个 多样性 推着[一般过去 bore] 我 向前，像 飓风一样，在 成功的 最初狂热中。生命和死亡 对我 显现为[一般过去] 理想的 界限，那个界限 我 应该 首先 冲破[情态 should + 原形]，并且 把 一股 光的洪流 倾注[省略 should + 原形] 进 我们黑暗的世界。一个 新物种 会 祝福[情态 would + 原形] 我，作为 它的创造者和源头；许多 快乐而优秀的 天性 会 欠[情态 would + 原形] 它们的存在 给 我。

palette.usage: bounds = limits, not boundaries on a map

palette.english: would owe their being to me
palette.chinese: 会把它们的存在归功于我

grammar: Which bore me onwards is a relative clause; its antecedent is variety of feelings.
Should first break through and pour share the same modal should.
The semicolon joins two consequences of creation: bless me and owe their being to me.
note: The English makes ambition sound luminous before it becomes dangerous; keep the grand verbs and modal future.
